article_title: Takeda's TAK-881 Matches HYQVIA in Pivotal Phase 2/3 Trial While Halving the Infusion Volume for Primary Immunodeficiency Patients
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Takeda's TAK-881 Matches HYQVIA in Pivotal Phase 2/3 Trial While Halving the Infusion Volume for Primary Immunodeficiency Patients
content_preview.summary: A 20% subcutaneous immunoglobulin paired with hyaluronidase delivered the same antibody exposure as HYQVIA in half the volume, clearing Takeda's path to global filings in fiscal 2026.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Takeda announced on May 4 that TAK-881-3001, a pivotal Phase 2/3 trial of its investigational subcutaneous immunoglobulin in patients with Primary Immunodeficiency Disease (PID), met its primary endpoint by demonstrating pharmacokinetic comparability with HYQVIA, the company's existing facilitated subcutaneous IG product, [according to BioSpace](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/takeda-announces-positive-topline-results-from-pivotal-phase-2-3-clinical-trial-of-tak-881-in-prima...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight Briefing piece, 537 words. Disciplined attribution: BioSpace carries the lead and most claims; Fierce Biotech is used only for the 99.67% ratio (corroborated) and the fiscal-2026-ends-March-2027 framing.

source_verification.source-0_biospace: Verified verbatim: TAK-881-3001 met primary endpoint, 99.67% geometric mean ratio (90% CI 95.10% to 104.46%), TAK-881 = SCIG 20% with recombinant human hyaluronidase, HYQVIA = SCIG 10%, Allikmets quote 'pharmacokinetic profile of TAK-881 was comparable to HYQVIA, an established IG standard of care in patients with PID, while offering the potential advantages of fewer injection sites, a flexible treatment schedule and shorter infusion times', Wasserman quote 'Patients needing lifelong IG therapy for PID experience a significant burden of care. Improving the administration process can diminish the burden of care by substantively impacting the treatment experience', trial design (51 weeks open-label cross-over for 16+, 27 weeks single-arm for 2-15), regulatory submission to US/EU/Japan in fiscal year 2026.
source_verification.source-1_fiercebiotech: Snapshot fetch returned HTTP 403 (bot blocked). The two claims attributed to Fierce Biotech — 99.67% AUC ratio and fiscal-2026-ends-March-2027 — are independently verified: the 99.67% ratio appears verbatim in BioSpace, and the fiscal-year math is mechanical (Takeda's fiscal year ends in March). The bot-block is a fetch artifact rather than a content concern.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All quoted material traced verbatim against the BioSpace snapshot. Fierce Biotech's contribution is corroborated by BioSpace, so its 403 fetch failure does not affect verifiability.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, precise clinical-trial reporting. The What We Don't Know section is honest about what the topline release didn't disclose (specific tolerability metrics, pricing, market launch timing).
editor_notes.originality: No prior TAK-881 / HYQVIA archive coverage. Fresh story.
concerns: fiercebiotech.com snapshot fetch returned HTTP 403 (bot block). The article's two Fierce-Biotech-attributed claims are corroborated by the BioSpace snapshot, so this is a fetch artifact rather than a content concern.
recommendations: Document the Fierce Biotech fetch artifact in a corrections file (advisory).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. Cryptography, content, and quoting discipline all clean. The single bot-block warning is a known fetch artifact for fiercebiotech.com, and the affected claims are corroborated by BioSpace.
